Commissiort 
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
(916) 322·5662 
Administration 
322-5660 
Execufive/legal 
322·5901 
Enforcement 
322-4441 
Stetements of Economic Interest 
322-6444 
Decemoer 30, 1983 
Assemblywoman Gloria Molina 
5261 Beverly Blvd. 
Los Angeles, CA 
90022 
Dear Assemolywoman Molina: 
Re: 
Advice Letter No. A-83-28I 
Thank you for your request for advice from our office. 
In 
your letter, you stated that the Southwest Voter Registration 
Education Project (SWVREP) of San Antonio, Texas, a non-profit 
organization, will be providing financial and technical 
assistance to register voters in California. 
One of the 
targeted areas is East Los Angeles, which comprises a major 
portion of your Assembly district. 
You have been asked to 
endorse the registration project, to send notices on your state 
letterhead, and to convene meetings with community leaders, in 
order to implement the voter registration drive. 
Your question 
is wnether your involvement in this project falls within the 
legal scope of your State Assemoly position. 
Your question is outside of the scope of the Political 
Reform Act and we suggest that you direct your question, 
instead, to tne Legislative Counsel's Office. 
The Political 
Reform Act would only be applicable if the SWVREP qualified as a 
committee and nad campaign reporting obligations. 
In order to 
qualify as a committee the SWVREP would have to make or receive 
payments that are for the purpose of influencing or attempting 
to influence the action of the voters for or against the 
nomination or election of a candidate or candidates or the 
qualification or passage of a measure. 
(Government Code Section 
82013, 2 Cal. Adm. Code Sections 18215 and 18225, copies 
enclosed). 
Based upon the facts that you have provided, this 
does not appear to be the case. 
You stated that SWVREP is 


Assemblywoman Gloria Molina 
December 30, 1983 
Page Two 
non-partisan in its efforts to register voters and you did not 
indicate that the group engages in any other activities which 
would qualify it as a committe~.l/ 
If you have any additional questions concerning this matter, 
please feel free to contact me at 916/322-5901. 
JSM:km 
Enclosures 
Very truly yours,

./7t:0 /t.-V1 v/uLr;..../.:,:"" /t-I /"--
.J Janis Shank McLean 
Counsel, Legal DiviSion 
1/ 
An example of such activities would be·the partisan. 
registration of voters. 


AEE 
<falifornia 1LtgiE 
GLORIA :v10LlNA 
December 15, 1983 
Ms. Barbara Milman 
Chief Of Legal Division 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P.o. BOX 807 
Sacramento, CA 95804 
Dear Barbara: 
This letter is to follow up on a telephone conversation with 
Judy Davis of your office on December 12. 
As was relayed to Ms. 
Davis, I am seeking the advice of your office regarding the following 
situation. 
The Southwest Voter Registration Education Project (SWVREP) of 
San Antonio, Texas has agreed to provide financial and technical 
assistance to register voters in California. 
One of the areas they 
have targeted is East Los Angeles. 
Since this covers a major portion 
of my District, I have been asked to endorse the project and serve 
as convener for meetings with community leaders to implement voter 
registration drives. 
It is important to note that the SWVREP is a non-profit 501-C-3 
orqanization and is, therefore, required to remain non partisan in 
their efforts 
to register voters. 
This organization has worked 
very successfully with other state legislatures in the past. 
I would like your 
advice on the the following: 
Does my involvement in this project fall 
within the legal scope of a State Assembly 
person (Which would include hosting meetings 
in my state office with community leaders and 
sending them notices of meetings on my state 
letterhead)? 
I would appreciate your response within the next week, if at all 
possible. 
Thank you for your assistance. 
If you would like further 
information on the SWVREP, please contact the Director of the Project 
Willie Velasquez, at (512) 222-0224. 
Sinc7rely, 
/(~fU( 
GLORIA MOLINA

sections.question: Does my involvement in this project fall within the legal scope of a State Assembly person (which would include hosting meetings in my state office with community leaders and sending them notices of meetings on my state letterhead)?
sections.conclusion: Your question is outside of the scope of the Political Reform Act and we suggest that you direct your question, instead, to the Legislative Counsel's Office. The Political Reform Act would only be applicable if the SWVREP qualified as a committee and had campaign reporting obligations. Based upon the facts that you have provided, this does not appear to be the case.
sections.question_synthetic: Assemblywoman Molina seeks FPPC advice on whether her participation in endorsing and facilitating a non-partisan voter registration project by SWVREP in her district falls within the legal scope of her State Assembly position.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that the question falls outside the Political Reform Act's scope and recommended referral to the Legislative Counsel's Office. The Political Reform Act applies only if SWVREP qualifies as a committee with campaign reporting obligations, which does not appear to be the case based on SWVREP's non-partisan voter registration activities.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request letter (December 15, 1983) and FPPC response letter (December 30, 1983). Response is clearly identified as Advice Letter No. A-83-281. Question and conclusion sections are explicitly framed in the respective documents.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does my involvement in this project fall within the legal scope of a State Assembly person (which would include hosting meetings in my state office with community leaders and sending them notices of meetings on my state letterhead)?

CONCLUSION: Your question is outside of the scope of the Political Reform Act and we suggest that you direct your question, instead, to the Legislative Counsel's Office. The Political Reform Act would only be applicable if the SWVREP qualified as a committee and had campaign reporting obligations. Based upon the facts that you have provided, this does not appear to be the case.

embedding.summary: Assemblywoman Gloria Molina requested FPPC advice on whether her involvement in a non-partisan voter registration project by SWVREP falls within her legal authority as a State Assembly member. The FPPC determined the question is outside its jurisdiction under the Political Reform Act and recommended she consult the Legislative Counsel's Office instead.
